channel.title: Last Day - NEW & UPDATE
channel.description: All NEW and UPDATE articles from the last day with enhanced metadata

item.title: NEW: CISA Mandates 3-Day Patch Window for Critical, Actively Exploited Ivanti Sentry Flaw
item.description:
Headline: CISA Mandates 3-Day Patch Window for Critical, Actively Exploited Ivanti Sentry Flaw

Summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued an emergency directive requiring federal agencies to patch a critical command injection vulnerability in Ivanti Sentry (CVE-2026-10520) by June 15, 2026. The flaw, which scores a perfect 10.0 on the CVSS scale, allows for unauthenticated remote code execution and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog after evidence of active exploitation emerged. Security researchers report observing widespread exploitation attempts against internet-exposed Sentry admin portals, warning that unpatched systems are likely already compromised.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: CISA has added the critical Ivanti Sentry vulnerability CVE-2026-10520 to its KEV catalog, mandating federal agencies patch the 10.0 CVSS flaw by June 15 due to active exploitation.

Article Slug: cisa-mandates-urgent-patch-for-actively-exploited-ivanti-sentry-flaw

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day, Breaching Over 100 Orgs, Primarily in Higher Education
item.description:
Headline: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day, Breaching Over 100 Orgs, Primarily in Higher Education

Summary:
The data extortion group ShinyHunters has been actively exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in Oracle's PeopleSoft Enterprise PeopleTools. The campaign, which took place before Oracle's patch release, has compromised over 100 organizations, with 68% being in the U.S. higher education sector. The 9.8 CVSS flaw allows for unauthenticated remote code execution. CISA has added the vulnerability to its KEV catalog, mandating a swift response from federal agencies. The University of Nottingham is one confirmed victim, with the attackers claiming to have stolen data on hundreds of thousands of students.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Threat Actor, Vulnerability, Data Breach

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: The ShinyHunters data extortion group is actively exploiting a critical zero-day RCE vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in Oracle PeopleSoft, compromising over 100 organizations, especially in the higher education sector.

Article Slug: shinyhunters-exploits-oracle-peoplesoft-zero-day-breaching-100-organizations

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Pro-Iranian Group 'Handala' Claims 'Warning' Attack on California Water Systems
item.description:
Headline: Pro-Iranian Group 'Handala' Claims 'Warning' Attack on California Water Systems

Summary:
The pro-Iranian hacktivist group 'Handala' has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against California water infrastructure. The group framed the intrusion as a retaliatory 'warning' to the U.S. for alleged strikes on Iranian water resources. While Handala asserted it did not disrupt water supplies, it released 5GB of data, including customer PII and administrative credentials for an RTKBase GPS network, as proof of the breach. The California Water Service (Cal Water), which serves two million customers, has been identified as a victim, with its Chico District confirmed to be affected.

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Industrial Control Systems

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: The pro-Iranian hacker group Handala has claimed a cyberattack on the California Water Service, leaking 5GB of data as a 'warning' while stating they did not disrupt water supplies.

Article Slug: pro-iranian-hackers-handala-claim-attack-on-california-water-systems

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Global Takedown Dismantles 'AudiA6' Crypto Laundering Service, Seizing €336M Pipeline for Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
Headline: Global Takedown Dismantles 'AudiA6' Crypto Laundering Service, Seizing €336M Pipeline for Ransomware Gangs

Summary:
An international law enforcement operation involving the U.S., Europe, and Georgia has dismantled 'AudiA6,' a major cryptocurrency laundering service responsible for washing over €336 million for ransomware gangs and other cybercriminals since 2021. The takedown resulted in the arrests of two senior administrators in Georgia, the seizure of servers and domains, and the shutdown of the associated 'Dark2Web' cybercrime forum. The service used a network of over 6,000 money mule accounts to obscure the origins of illicit funds, with blockchain analysis linking it to numerous major cybercrime investigations, including the 2022 LastPass breach.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence, Ransomware

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: An international law enforcement operation has dismantled the 'AudiA6' cryptocurrency laundering service, which processed over €336 million for ransomware gangs, leading to arrests and infrastructure seizures.

Article Slug: international-law-enforcement-dismantles-audia6-crypto-laundering-service

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Conti Ransomware Coder Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court for Wire Fraud Conspiracy
item.description:
Headline: Conti Ransomware Coder Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court for Wire Fraud Conspiracy

Summary:
Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko, a 44-year-old Ukrainian national, has pleaded guilty in a U.S. federal court to conspiracy to commit wire fraud. He admitted to his role in the notorious Conti ransomware gang, which was responsible for over $150 million in ransom payments. Lytvynenko, who was arrested in Ireland and extradited, confessed to joining the group in 2021 and developing a malware 'loader' used to deploy the ransomware payload in attacks on at least twelve victims. He faces up to 20 years in prison.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Security Operations

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko, a Ukrainian national, pleaded guilty for his role as a malware developer for the Conti ransomware group, admitting to creating a loader used in their global cybercrime campaign.

Article Slug: ukrainian-national-pleads-guilty-for-role-in-conti-ransomware-conspiracy

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Pharma Giant Novo Nordisk Discloses Data Breach Affecting Clinical Trial Participants
item.description:
Headline: Pharma Giant Novo Nordisk Discloses Data Breach Affecting Clinical Trial Participants

Summary:
Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk, maker of popular drugs Ozempic and Wegovy, has announced a data breach involving unauthorized access to its IT systems. Attackers copied and exfiltrated data related to the company's clinical trials. For trial participants, the exposed data was pseudonymized (including patient IDs, health info, and lifestyle factors), but for healthcare professionals, it included directly identifiable information like names, email addresses, and phone numbers. Novo Nordisk has taken some systems offline and is warning affected parties to be vigilant against targeted phishing attacks.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Phishing, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: Pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk has disclosed a data breach where attackers accessed and copied sensitive data related to clinical trial participants and healthcare professionals.

Article Slug: novo-nordisk-discloses-data-breach-affecting-clinical-trial-participants

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: South Korea Hits Coupang with Record $409M Fine for 'Negligent' Data Breach Affecting 37.5M People
item.description:
Headline: South Korea Hits Coupang with Record $409M Fine for 'Negligent' Data Breach Affecting 37.5M People

Summary:
South Korea's Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) has levied a historic fine of approximately $409 million against e-commerce giant Coupang for a data breach that exposed the personal information of 37.56 million people. The regulator attributed the breach, which occurred in 2025, to 'negligent management' and 'deficiencies in basic safety management,' not a sophisticated hack. The exposed data included information on 33.22 million members and 4.34 million non-members. Coupang was also cited for unlawfully collecting user browsing data and obstructing the investigation.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory, Policy and Compliance

Article Type: NewsArticle

Meta Description: South Korean e-commerce giant Coupang has been fined a record $409 million for a massive data breach affecting 37.5 million people, which regulators attributed to negligent security practices.

Article Slug: south-korea-imposes-record-409m-fine-on-coupang-for-massive-data-breach

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers
item.description:
Headline: 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers

Summary:
A sophisticated supply chain attack dubbed 'Atomic Arch' is targeting Linux developers by hijacking trusted but orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR). Attackers claim ownership of these abandoned packages and modify their build scripts (PKGBUILD) to fetch and install a malicious npm package. This secondary payload, 'atomic-lockfile', deploys a Rust-based infostealer and an eBPF rootkit designed to harvest developer credentials, tokens, and SSH keys. The campaign subverts the AUR's trust model and has compromised over 20 packages in multiple waves.

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: Analysis

Meta Description: A new supply chain attack called 'Atomic Arch' is targeting Linux developers by hijacking orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) to distribute credential-stealing malware.

Article Slug: atomic-arch-supply-chain-attack-hijacks-orphaned-linux-packages

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: New 'GreatXML' Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker Encryption via Defender Offline Scan Feature
item.description:
Headline: New 'GreatXML' Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker Encryption via Defender Offline Scan Feature

Summary:
A security researcher has disclosed a new zero-day exploit named 'GreatXML' that can bypass Windows BitLocker drive encryption. The attack requires physical access to the target machine and leverages an interaction with the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) and the Microsoft Defender Offline Scan feature. By planting malicious XML files in the recovery partition, an attacker can trigger the offline scan to spawn a SYSTEM-level command prompt, granting full access to the encrypted C: drive. The exploit was demonstrated on Windows 11 24H2, but its real-world impact is considered limited due to the specific prerequisites.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: TechArticle

Meta Description: A new zero-day exploit called 'GreatXML' has been disclosed that can bypass Windows BitLocker drive encryption, requiring physical access and leveraging the Defender Offline Scan feature.

Article Slug: new-greatxml-exploit-bypasses-windows-bitlocker-encryption

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: FinCEN Issues New Guidance to Encourage Banks to Share Fraud-Related Information
item.description:
Headline: FinCEN Issues New Guidance to Encourage Banks to Share Fraud-Related Information

Summary:
The U.S. Treasury's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) has released updated guidance to encourage financial institutions to voluntarily share information related to suspected fraud. The guidance clarifies that the safe harbor provisions of Section 314(b) of the USA PATRIOT Act apply to the sharing of fraud-related information, not just money laundering and terrorist financing. This move aims to help banks work together to identify and stop fraud, which costs the U.S. hundreds of billions annually, by allowing them to share cyber intelligence like IP addresses and other fraud indicators.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: The U.S. Treasury's FinCEN has issued new guidance clarifying that financial institutions can share fraud-related information under the safe harbor of the USA PATRIOT Act's Section 314(b).

Article Slug: fincen-issues-new-guidance-to-banks-on-sharing-fraud-related-information

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: White House Overhauls Cybersecurity Policy for National Security Systems with NSPM-12
item.description:
Headline: White House Overhauls Cybersecurity Policy for National Security Systems with NSPM-12

Summary:
On June 12, 2026, President Donald J. Trump signed National Security Presidential Memorandum (NSPM-12), a new directive to overhaul the cybersecurity governance for America's most sensitive National Security Systems (NSS). The memo re-establishes the Committee on National Security Systems (CNSS) after 35 years and formally designates the NSA Director as the National Manager for NSS. This empowers the NSA to set baseline security requirements and provide direct assistance to secure classified and mission-critical systems across the entire U.S. government, including civilian agencies.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: President Trump has signed NSPM-12, a new directive to modernize cybersecurity for U.S. National Security Systems, empowering the NSA as the National Manager to set binding requirements.

Article Slug: us-government-modernizes-cybersecurity-policy-for-national-security-systems

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Stolen Credentials as Top Breach Vector, Verizon DBIR Finds
item.description:
Headline: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Stolen Credentials as Top Breach Vector, Verizon DBIR Finds

Summary:
The 2026 Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) reveals a major shift in the threat landscape: for the first time, the exploitation of software vulnerabilities (31%) has surpassed the use of stolen credentials (13%) as the primary initial access vector in data breaches. The report highlights a concerning trend of declining patch rates for critical vulnerabilities, with organizations taking longer to remediate known exploited flaws. Ransomware remains a dominant threat, present in 48% of all breaches, although the report notes a slight decrease in both ransom payment rates and median payment amounts.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Data Breach, Vulnerability

Article Type: Report

Meta Description: The 2026 Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) shows that for the first time, vulnerability exploitation is the leading cause of data breaches, surpassing stolen credentials.

Article Slug: verizon-dbir-2026-highlights-shift-to-vulnerability-exploitation

Published Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Now the #1 Path to Data Breaches
item.description:
Headline: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Now the #1 Path to Data Breaches

Summary:
The 2026 Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) has revealed a major shift in the cyber threat landscape: for the first time in 19 years, the exploitation of vulnerabilities is the leading initial access vector for data breaches, accounting for 31% of incidents. This surpasses stolen credentials, which have long dominated as the primary entry point. The report, which analyzed over 22,000 breaches, attributes this trend to organizations' struggles with vulnerability management and the speed at which attackers can weaponize new flaws. Alarmingly, the report also found a decline in patch rates for critical vulnerabilities, with only 26% of flaws in CISA's KEV catalog being fully remediated in 2025, down from 38% the previous year. This data signals a critical need for organizations to prioritize and improve their vulnerability management and patching programs.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Patch Management

Meta Description: The 2026 Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR) reveals that vulnerability exploitation has surpassed stolen credentials as the top initial access vector in data breaches for the first time.

Article Slug: verizon-2026-dbir-vulnerability-exploits-surpass-stolen-credentials

Published Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 20 May 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Ivanti Patches Critical Sentry Flaws Allowing Root-Level RCE
item.description:
Headline: Ivanti Patches Critical Sentry Flaws Allowing Root-Level RCE

Summary:
Ivanti has released urgent security updates for two critical vulnerabilities in its Ivanti Sentry (formerly MobileIron Sentry) product. The most severe flaw, CVE-2026-10520, is an unauthenticated OS command injection vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to achieve root-level remote code execution on the appliance. A second flaw, CVE-2026-10523, permits authentication bypass. While there is no evidence of active exploitation, security researchers have already published technical details and a proof-of-concept scanner for CVE-2026-10520, significantly increasing the risk of attack. Given that Sentry appliances act as gateways to sensitive corporate resources, compromise could be devastating. All users are urged to update to the patched versions immediately.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Mobile Security

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: Ivanti has patched two critical vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-10520, CVE-2026-10523) in Ivanti Sentry that allow for unauthenticated remote code execution and auth bypass. Patch immediately.

Article Slug: ivanti-sentry-critical-vulnerabilities-allow-root-rce

Published Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Researcher Drops 'GreatXML' Zero-Day Exploit to Bypass Windows BitLocker
item.description:
Headline: Researcher Drops 'GreatXML' Zero-Day Exploit to Bypass Windows BitLocker

Summary:
A security researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse has publicly released details of 'GreatXML,' a new unpatched zero-day exploit that allegedly bypasses Windows BitLocker encryption. The exploit allows an attacker with physical access to a machine to gain SYSTEM-level privileges in the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) by abusing XML artifacts left by a Microsoft Defender offline scan. The disclosure is part of an ongoing dispute between the researcher and Microsoft over its bug bounty program.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor

Article Type: TechArticle

Meta Description: A researcher has released 'GreatXML,' a new zero-day exploit that reportedly bypasses Windows BitLocker by abusing the Windows Recovery Environment (WinRE) on systems that have used Microsoft Defender's offline scan.

Article Slug: new-greatxml-zero-day-bypasses-windows-bitlocker

Published Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Oracle Rushes Emergency Patch for PeopleSoft Zero-Day Exploited by ShinyHunters
item.description:
Headline: Oracle Rushes Emergency Patch for PeopleSoft Zero-Day Exploited by ShinyHunters

Summary:
Oracle has released an emergency, out-of-band patch for a critical unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability in its PeopleSoft PeopleTools software, tracked as CVE-2026-35273. The urgent release follows confirmation that the flaw is being actively exploited in the wild by the ShinyHunters extortion group. The attackers are reportedly targeting both on-premises and cloud instances, with a focus on the education sector, to steal data for extortion.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: Oracle has released an emergency patch for a critical RCE zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-35273) in PeopleSoft PeopleTools, which is being actively exploited by the ShinyHunters group in data theft attacks.

Article Slug: oracle-issues-emergency-patch-for-peoplesoft-zero-day-cve-2026-35273

Published Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Sat, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Leaked, Spawns 'Hades' Variant Targeting Microsoft and PyPI
item.description:
Headline: Miasma Supply Chain Worm Leaked, Spawns 'Hades' Variant Targeting Microsoft and PyPI

Summary:
The 'Miasma' supply chain attack framework, a credential-stealing toolkit, was leaked on GitHub, rapidly evolving into a new worm variant dubbed 'Hades'. This new threat has compromised numerous Microsoft GitHub repositories, including Azure projects, and 19 packages on the Python Package Index (PyPI). The attack introduces a novel trigger mechanism, executing a credential-harvesting payload when a developer simply opens a compromised project in a popular AI-assisted IDE. The campaign has already led to the disabling of 73 Microsoft repositories, disrupting CI/CD pipelines globally, and has affected hundreds of packages across npm and PyPI since early June 2026.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 7 minutes

Meta Description: A leaked supply chain attack framework called Miasma has evolved into the Hades worm, compromising Microsoft's GitHub and the PyPI package index with a novel IDE-based execution vector.

Article Slug: miasma-supply-chain-worm-spreads-to-microsoft-and-pypi-hades-variant

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Microsoft's Largest-Ever Patch Tuesday Addresses 206 Flaws, Including 3 Zero-Days
item.description:
Headline: Microsoft's Largest-Ever Patch Tuesday Addresses 206 Flaws, Including 3 Zero-Days

Summary:
Microsoft has released its largest security update in history for the June 2026 Patch Tuesday, addressing a total of 206 vulnerabilities across its software portfolio. The massive update includes patches for 33 critical flaws and three zero-day vulnerabilities that were publicly known before the fixes were available. Key zero-days include a BitLocker security feature bypass (CVE-2026-50507), an HTTP.sys denial-of-service flaw (CVE-2026-49160), and a Windows privilege escalation bug (CVE-2026-45586). While none are reported as actively exploited, their public disclosure elevates the urgency for organizations to apply these critical updates.

Severity: High

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday is the largest in history, addressing 206 vulnerabilities, including 33 critical flaws and three publicly disclosed zero-days affecting BitLocker, HTTP.sys, and Windows.

Article Slug: microsoft-june-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-206-vulnerabilities-3-zero-days

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Qilin Ransomware Claims Attacks on U.S. Law Firm and Industrial Company
item.description:
Headline: Qilin Ransomware Claims Attacks on U.S. Law Firm and Industrial Company

Summary:
The prolific Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for cyberattacks against two U.S.-based organizations: the law firm Dulany Leahy Curtis & Brophy and the industrial machinery company JV Equipment. The threat actors posted the claims on their dark web leak site on June 10 and June 11, 2026, respectively. Employing double extortion tactics, the group is threatening to publish sensitive data stolen from both victims if they do not enter into ransom negotiations. These incidents underscore Qilin's continued focus on a diverse range of sectors.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Data Breach

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for cyberattacks on U.S. law firm Dulany Leahy Curtis & Brophy and industrial firm JV Equipment, threatening to leak data.

Article Slug: qilin-ransomware-group-targets-us-law-firm-and-industrial-company

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: 'Gentlemen' Ransomware, a Qilin Splinter, Hits 478 Victims in Worm-Like Attacks
item.description:
Headline: 'Gentlemen' Ransomware, a Qilin Splinter, Hits 478 Victims in Worm-Like Attacks

Summary:
A new and highly aggressive ransomware strain named 'Gentlemen' has compromised at least 478 victims across 66 countries. Believed to be a splinter group from the notorious Qilin RaaS operation and tracked by Microsoft as Storm-2697, the group employs worm-like capabilities to encrypt entire networks rapidly. The primary attack vector is the active exploitation of vulnerabilities in Fortinet FortiGate appliances and VPNs, including CVE-2025-32433 and CVE-2025-33073. The Go-based ransomware is heavily obfuscated, and the group uses a double extortion model, targeting industries like IT services, manufacturing, and finance, with a particular focus on Southeast Asia and Latin America.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Vulnerability

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: A new worm-like ransomware strain called 'Gentlemen,' a splinter from the Qilin group, has rapidly compromised 478 organizations by exploiting Fortinet vulnerabilities.

Article Slug: gentlemen-ransomware-qilin-splinter-hits-478-victims-exploiting-fortinet-flaws

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Canadian Regulator Warns of Risks from Advanced AI in Cybersecurity
item.description:
Headline: Canadian Regulator Warns of Risks from Advanced AI in Cybersecurity

Summary:
The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO) has issued a cybersecurity alert to its members regarding the dual-use nature of frontier Artificial Intelligence (AI) models. The bulletin, published on June 11, 2026, warns that while advanced AI can enhance cyber defenses, it also significantly increases risk by enabling malicious actors to discover and exploit vulnerabilities at an accelerated pace. CIRO advises financial firms to reassess their cybersecurity frameworks, particularly their vulnerability management and incident response processes, to adapt to this new, faster-paced threat landscape.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Article Type: Advisory

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO) has issued a cybersecurity alert warning that advanced AI models are accelerating vulnerability discovery, increasing risks for the financial sector.

Article Slug: canadian-regulator-warns-of-cyber-risks-from-frontier-ai-models

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: DXC and Anthropic Form Alliance to Deploy AI in Enterprise Systems
item.description:
Headline: DXC and Anthropic Form Alliance to Deploy AI in Enterprise Systems

Summary:
IT services giant DXC Technology and AI safety company Anthropic have announced a major global partnership. The multi-year alliance will focus on integrating Anthropic's Claude AI models into the complex, mission-critical systems DXC manages for its enterprise clients in sectors like banking, insurance, and government. A key focus will be cybersecurity, where DXC plans to use Claude as a 'security engineer sub-agent' within its DXC OASIS security platform to enhance cyber resilience in its Security Operations Centers (SOCs). DXC reported that using Claude has already accelerated its own software development by a factor of ten.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: DXC Technology and AI company Anthropic have formed a global partnership to integrate the Claude AI model into mission-critical enterprise systems, with a key focus on cybersecurity applications.

Article Slug: dxc-technology-and-anthropic-form-alliance-for-enterprise-ai

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: DHS Cyber Modernization Efforts Face Hurdles, GAO Report Finds
item.description:
Headline: DHS Cyber Modernization Efforts Face Hurdles, GAO Report Finds

Summary:
A new report from the U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO) finds that while major cybersecurity programs at the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) are strengthening federal cyber defenses, they face significant hurdles. The June 12, 2026 report highlights challenges related to mounting costs, staffing shortages, and the difficulty of keeping pace with an evolving threat landscape. Key programs like Continuous Diagnostics and Mitigation (CDM) and CyberSentry are advancing, but require sustained investment and adaptation to remain effective against increasingly sophisticated cyber threats targeting federal networks and critical infrastructure.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Article Type: Report

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: A U.S. GAO report finds that DHS cybersecurity programs like CDM are bolstering federal resilience but face significant challenges from costs, staffing, and a rapidly evolving threat landscape.

Article Slug: dhs-cyber-modernization-faces-challenges-gao-report

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Europol Dismantles 'AudiA6' Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
Headline: Europol Dismantles 'AudiA6' Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs

Summary:
An international law enforcement operation led by Europol has dismantled 'AudiA6,' a major cryptocurrency laundering service suspected of washing over €336 million for ransomware gangs and other cybercriminals between 2022 and 2025. The service, marketed on underground forums, was linked to over 15 global cybercrime investigations. Coordinated action on June 10, 2026, resulted in the arrest of two administrators in Georgia, the takedown of 25 domains, and the seizure of over 30 servers and €692,000 in cryptocurrency, dealing a significant blow to the financial infrastructure of cybercrime.

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 4 minutes

Meta Description: An international operation led by Europol has dismantled the 'AudiA6' cryptocurrency laundering service, which is suspected of washing over €336 million for ransomware gangs.

Article Slug: europol-dismantles-audia6-crypto-laundering-service-for-ransomware-gangs

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Global Schools Foundation Suffers Major Data Breach, Exposing 33,000 Passports
item.description:
Headline: Global Schools Foundation Suffers Major Data Breach, Exposing 33,000 Passports

Summary:
The Global Schools Foundation (GSF), an international K-12 education organization, has been impacted by a significant data breach attributed to the threat actor 'FulcrumSec'. The incident, reported on June 11, 2026, resulted in the exposure of highly sensitive personal information of both students and employees. Most alarmingly, the compromised data includes 33,088 passport numbers, creating a severe risk of identity theft and fraud for the thousands of individuals affected across GSF's international network of schools.

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: The Global Schools Foundation (GSF) has suffered a major data breach attributed to threat actor FulcrumSec, exposing sensitive information including 33,088 passport numbers.

Article Slug: global-schools-foundation-suffers-major-data-breach-exposing-passports

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Team Cymru Expands Asia-Pacific Operations to Meet Threat Intel Demand
item.description:
Headline: Team Cymru Expands Asia-Pacific Operations to Meet Threat Intel Demand

Summary:
Threat intelligence firm Team Cymru has announced a major expansion of its operations in the Asia-Pacific and Japan (APJ) region, naming Sydney, Australia, as its new regional hub. The move, announced on June 12, 2026, is driven by increasing demand from critical infrastructure, financial services, and government sectors for greater visibility into the external threat landscape. Team Cymru stated that the expansion will help regional organizations defend against well-resourced adversaries by providing proactive insights into attacker infrastructure via its 'Pure Signal' platform.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Security Operations

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 3 minutes

Meta Description: Threat intelligence firm Team Cymru is expanding its Asia-Pacific and Japan (APJ) operations by establishing a new regional hub in Sydney, Australia, to meet growing demand.

Article Slug: team-cymru-expands-asia-pacific-operations-with-sydney-hub

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: NEW: Ontinue Awarded 'Most Innovative XDR Platform' for AI-Driven Security
item.description:
Headline: Ontinue Awarded 'Most Innovative XDR Platform' for AI-Driven Security

Summary:
Ontinue, a provider of Managed Extended Detection and Response (MXDR), has won the 'Most Innovative XDR Platform' award in the 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards by The Hacker News. The award, announced on June 12, 2026, recognizes Ontinue's ION platform and its 'Agentic SOC' model. This model is designed for what the company calls the 'Autonomous Threat Era' and combines agentic AI for autonomous investigation with human oversight. The approach aims to counter the increasing speed and scale of AI-driven cyberattacks by automating security operations and accelerating response times.

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 3 minutes

Meta Description: Ontinue's ION MXDR platform has been named the 'Most Innovative XDR Platform' for its 'Agentic SOC' model, which uses AI to automate security operations and combat AI-driven threats.

Article Slug: ontinue-awarded-most-innovative-xdr-platform-for-ai-driven-security

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Novo Nordisk Confirms Data Breach, Urges Vigilance from Trial Participants
item.description:
Headline: Novo Nordisk Confirms Data Breach, Urges Vigilance from Trial Participants

Summary:
Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk announced on June 11, 2026, that it suffered a cybersecurity incident where attackers gained unauthorized access to internal IT systems. The company confirmed that personal data related to some clinical trial participants was accessed. While Novo Nordisk stated the data was not directly linked to patient names, it did include sensitive information such as sex, year of birth, and biomarkers. The company has taken some systems offline as a precaution and is urging trial participants to be vigilant for any unusual activity.

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Cyberattack, Regulatory

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 5 minutes

Meta Description: Pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk has confirmed a cybersecurity breach that exposed personal data of some clinical trial participants, urging them to remain vigilant.

Article Slug: novo-nordisk-confirms-data-breach-affecting-clinical-trial-participants

Published Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 12:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Deploys Self-Propagating Malware in Global RaaS Campaign
item.description:
Headline: 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Deploys Self-Propagating Malware in Global RaaS Campaign

Summary:
Microsoft has uncovered a highly sophisticated Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) operation named 'The Gentlemen,' tracked as Storm-2697. This financially motivated group has developed a Go-based ransomware that features robust encryption and, most notably, an aggressive self-propagation module designed to autonomously spread across enterprise networks. The group, which recently partnered with the BreachForums marketplace to recruit affiliates, employs a double-extortion model, targeting organizations in critical sectors like healthcare, finance, and transportation across multiple continents. The malware is heavily obfuscated and designed to disable security tools before encryption.

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Malware

Meta Description: Microsoft details 'The Gentlemen' (Storm-2697), a new Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) threat with a self-propagating Go-based encryptor targeting global industries.

Article Slug: the-gentlemen-ransomware-emerges-with-self-propagation-and-raas-model

Published Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 01 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Miasma Worm Breaches 73 Microsoft GitHub Repos in AI-Powered Supply Chain Attack
item.description:
Headline: Miasma Worm Breaches 73 Microsoft GitHub Repos in AI-Powered Supply Chain Attack

Summary:
The 'Miasma worm,' a self-replicating malware, has executed a significant supply chain attack, compromising 73 GitHub repositories across four major Microsoft organizations. The attack, which occurred on June 5, 2026, leveraged a previously compromised contributor account to inject a credential-harvesting payload. This payload was specifically designed to activate when developers used popular AI-powered coding assistants like Claude Code and VS Code, stealing credentials to propagate itself further. The incident is believed to be an evolution of the 'Mini Shai-Hulud' worm, publicly released by the threat group TeamPCP. GitHub responded swiftly by disabling the affected repositories, but the attack highlights the growing threat of AI-assisted malware and the targeting of core developer infrastructure.

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Article Type: NewsArticle

Reading Time: 6 minutes

Meta Description: A detailed analysis of the Miasma worm, a self-replicating supply chain attack that compromised 73 Microsoft GitHub repositories by leveraging AI coding tools to harvest developer credentials.

Article Slug: miasma-worm-supply-chain-attack-microsoft-github

Published Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Mon, 08 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →

item.title: UPDATE: Microsoft's Record-Breaking June Patch Tuesday Fixes 206 Flaws, Including 3 Zero-Days
item.description:
Headline: Microsoft's Record-Breaking June Patch Tuesday Fixes 206 Flaws, Including 3 Zero-Days

Summary:
Microsoft has released its largest security update in history for June 2026, fixing a total of 206 vulnerabilities across its product ecosystem. The massive release includes patches for 32 critical flaws and addresses three publicly disclosed zero-day vulnerabilities affecting Windows BitLocker, HTTP.sys, and the Windows Collaborative Translation Framework. While none of the zero-days are reported as actively exploited, their public nature elevates the urgency for organizations to apply the updates immediately.

Severity: Critical

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability

Article Type: Advisory

Meta Description: Microsoft's June 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses a historic 206 vulnerabilities, including three publicly disclosed zero-days (CVE-2026-50507, CVE-2026-49160, CVE-2026-45586). Learn about the critical fixes and why immediate patching is essential.

Article Slug: microsoft-june-2026-patch-tuesday-fixes-206-vulnerabilities-three-zero-days

Published Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Created Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 15:00:00 GMT

Updated Date: Fri, 12 Jun 2026 00:00:00 GMT

Update Count: 1

Update History: 1 update recorded

Read Full Article →
